news.headline: Caribou Biosciences outpaces medical sector year-to-date, holds Zacks Rank #2 (Buy)
news.summary: With shares around $1.93 and a market cap near $192M, CRBU is up roughly 22% year-to-date, well ahead of the ~1.5% average for the Medical sector. Estimate revisions have pushed the full-year consensus about 11% higher over the past quarter, reflecting improving analyst sentiment after the EHA readouts.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences added to Russell 2000 and Russell Microcap indices
news.summary: CRBU was added to the reconstituted Russell 2000, Russell Microcap and related value benchmark indices at the June 2026 rebalance. Index inclusion brings incremental passive fund ownership, a modest structural tailwind for this thinly traded micro-cap.

news.headline: Caribou shareholders reject officer exculpation proposal at annual meeting
news.summary: At its 2026 annual meeting Caribou shareholders elected the director slate and ratified the auditor but voted down a proposed charter amendment extending liability exculpation to corporate officers. A governance signal reflecting some investor pushback rather than a fundamental driver.

news.headline: H.C. Wainwright reiterates Buy rating on Caribou Biosciences
news.summary: H.C. Wainwright reaffirmed a Buy rating following the EHA 2026 datasets, echoing Citi's Buy and $8 target. The sell side broadly views the vispa-cel and CB-011 results as validating Caribou's off-the-shelf CRISPR CAR-T, though targets embed successful financing of the pivotal program.

news.headline: Caribou reports long-term vispa-cel (CB-010) data in second-line large B-cell lymphoma at EHA 2026
news.summary: Updated ANTLER data for vispa-cel (formerly CB-010) showed roughly 82% ORR, 67% CR and a 17.1-month median PFS in optimized-dose second-line LBCL patients, with no GvHD and minimal severe CRS. The durability supports Caribou's case that its allogeneic product can match approved autologous CAR-T ahead of the planned ANTLER-3 pivotal Phase 3.

news.headline: Caribou reports CB-011 dose-escalation durability data at EHA 2026
news.summary: CB-011 (anti-BCMA) posted a roughly 92% ORR, 75% or greater CR and 91% MRD negativity in BCMA-naive relapsed/refractory myeloma patients at the recommended expansion dose, with durable complete responses. The clean efficacy and safety profile positions the RMAT-designated program as pivotal-ready and is Caribou's most advanced myeloma catalyst.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings & business update
catalysts.detail: Company is expected to report around August 11, 2026, with updated cash position and, importantly, any commentary on financing the vispa-cel pivotal trial.

catalysts.label: vispa-cel ANTLER-3 pivotal Phase 3
catalysts.detail: FDA alignment on trial design has been reached; ANTLER-3 is a randomized ~250-patient Phase 3 in CD19-naive 2L LBCL (transplant/autologous-ineligible). Initiation is contingent on securing capital or a partner, which management calls mission-critical for global reach.

catalysts.label: CB-011 CaMMouflage expansion data
catalysts.detail: Additional durability and MRD-negativity data from the RMAT-designated anti-BCMA myeloma program could support an accelerated regulatory path.

catalysts.label: Cash runway limit
catalysts.detail: Cash, equivalents and marketable securities fund the current operating plan (incl. CB-011 dose expansion and vispa-cel pivotal start-up activities) into 2H 2027 (cash balance ~$118.6M as of the most recent quarter), making a financing or partnership the key near-term catalyst and overhang.

catalysts.label: Investor conference appearances
catalysts.detail: Management continues presenting at cell-therapy and healthcare conferences (e.g. H.C. Wainwright, Jefferies), which can support awareness and partnering discussions.

pulse: Sentiment remains cautiously constructive heading into the August 11 Q2 earnings print: vispa-cel (~82% ORR, 17.1-month PFS in 2L LBCL) and CB-011 (~92% ORR, 91% MRD negativity in myeloma) data from EHA 2026 continue to support the sell side's bullish stance, with CB-011 holding FDA RMAT designation and ANTLER-3's pivotal design already aligned with the FDA. Analysts (H.C. Wainwright, Citi) have reiterated Buy ratings, CRBU carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) as of July 12, and the stock's recent Russell 2000/Microcap index inclusion added a modest structural tailwind; the stock is up roughly 22% YTD near $1.93 (~$192M market cap), outperforming the medical sector. The dominant overhang remains financing: with cash (~$118.6M) providing runway only into 2H 2027, the ~250-patient ANTLER-3 pivotal trial depends on raising capital or landing a partner, keeping dilution risk in focus and the setup high-beta and binary.
